description: Used when a journey is split into two equal distance segments traveled at speeds x and y.
keyInsight: The average speed is the harmonic mean of the individual speeds, not the arithmetic mean. If the segments are equal time, then average speed is (x + y)/2.

formula: Relative Speed = x + y (opposite direction) | x - y (same direction)
description: Applies when two bodies are moving relative to each other.
keyInsight: Add speeds if moving towards/away from each other. Subtract if one is chasing the other.

description: If A takes 'a' days and B takes 'b' days, working together they take T days.
keyInsight: Work done is inversely proportional to time taken. Represent rates of work per day as fractions.

formula: Sum of roots = -b/a | Product of roots = c/a
description: For any quadratic equation ax^2 + bx + c = 0.
keyInsight: Discriminant D = b^2 - 4ac determines root nature: D > 0 (real & distinct), D = 0 (real & equal), D < 0 (complex).

formula: log_a(x * y) = log_a(x) + log_a(y) | log_a(x/y) = log_a(x) - log_a(y)
keyInsight: Base change theorem: log_a(b) = log_c(b) / log_c(a). Crucial for algebraic simplifications.

description: Sum of first n terms of an Arithmetic Progression.
keyInsight: Can also be written as n * (First term + Last term) / 2. The average of terms is the middle term.

description: Sum of an infinite Geometric Progression.
keyInsight: If common ratio r is not fractional (|r| >= 1), the sum diverges to infinity.

formula: Area = sqrt(s * (s - a) * (s - b) * (s - c)) where s = (a + b + c) / 2
description: Heron's formula for the area of any triangle given its three side lengths.
keyInsight: Useful when heights are not given, but side lengths are known integers (Heronian triangles).
Sum of exterior angles is always 360° regardless of the number of sides.

formula: phi(N) = N * (1 - 1/p1) * (1 - 1/p2)... where p1, p2 are prime factors
description: Euler's totient function counts numbers coprime to N up to N.
keyInsight: If a is coprime to N, then a^(phi(N)) mod N = 1 (Euler's theorem). Simplifies massive exponents in remainders.

formula: Cyclicity of unit digits: 2, 3, 7, 8 have cyclicity of 4 | 4, 9 have cyclicity of 2 | 0, 1, 5, 6 have cyclicity of 1
description: Finding the last digit of any number raised to a large power.
keyInsight: Divide the exponent by the cyclicity. The remainder gives the power step. If remainder is 0, use cyclicity itself.

description: Number of ways to select r items from a set of n distinct items.
keyInsight: nCr = nC(n-r). Remember that order of selection does not matter in combinations.

description: Number of ways to arrange n distinct objects along a circle.
keyInsight: If clockwise and counter-clockwise arrangements are considered identical (like a necklace), it is (n - 1)! / 2.
